commit.message:
docs: team consensus on tool priorities — voted and assigned

Team voting ceremony completed. All 25 tools from Jango's proposal
prioritized by squad consensus based on:
- Which M1+M2 blockers each tool would have prevented
- Which agents explicitly requested similar functionality
- Impact on developer satisfaction (8/10 cited 'never saw my work')

Sprint breakdown:
- Sprint A (7 tools): Critical blockers prevention, before M3
- Sprint B (6 tools): Developer joy, during M3
- Sprint C (8 tools): Process automation, post M3
- Backlog (4 tools): Deferred

Top 3 priorities:
1. Integration Gate Automation (#33) — 17 points
2. Godot Headless Validator (#35) — 15 points
3. Branch Validation CI (#34) — 14 points

Key decision: Test Scene Generator moved from Sprint B to Sprint A
due to overwhelming team demand (80% reported satisfaction gap).

Next: Create remaining 18 GitHub issues for Sprint A/B/C tools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add missing Invalidate() calls to FileInfo state-changing methods (#124429)

Fixes #117709 — FileInfo methods that alter file system state now call
Invalidate() so cached properties (Exists, Length, timestamps) are
lazily refreshed on next access, matching the pattern already used by
Create(), Delete(), and MoveTo().

Methods fixed (FileInfo.cs):
  - Open(FileStreamOptions)
  - Open(FileMode, FileAccess, FileShare)
  - OpenRead()
  - OpenWrite()
  - OpenText()
  - Replace(string, string?)
  - Encrypt()
  - Decrypt()
  - CreateAsHardLink(string)

Comprehensive method audit across FileInfo, DirectoryInfo,
FileSystemInfo:

  FileInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    Create(), Delete(), MoveTo(), CreateText(), AppendText(),
    CopyTo() (returns new obj), IsReadOnly setter (via Attributes)

  DirectoryInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    Create(), Delete(), Delete(bool), MoveTo()
    CreateSubdirectory() returns new obj, parent unchanged

  FileSystemInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    CreateAsSymbolicLink(), Attributes setter, CreationTime(Utc) setter,
    LastAccessTime(Utc) setter, LastWriteTime(Utc) setter,
    UnixFileMode setter, ResolveLinkTarget() (read-only)

  Read-only methods (no invalidation needed):
    GetFiles, GetDirectories, GetFileSystemInfos, Enumerate*

Includes 12 regression tests in InvalidateOnStateChange.cs covering all
fixed methods plus CreateText/AppendText (already worked).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: direct polygon passthrough for table row citation highlighting

Instead of relying on fragile geometry-distribution + text-matching
pipeline to link table row polygons, cell bounding_region polygons
are now extracted and normalized directly in _extract_table_metadata()
and stored alongside each row dict as _polygons/_page.

extract_sentences_from_di_units() Source B reads _polygons directly,
bypassing _match_geometry_for_sentence(). The text-matching path is
retained as fallback. Warnings logged when neither path provides data.

All row.items() consumers (schema_aware_extractor, synthesis) filter
_-prefixed internal keys to prevent noise in LLM context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: Colima/OrbStack do NOT support ASAN on Apple Silicon

Docker Desktop (Rosetta 2) is the only macOS ARM64 Docker runtime that
supports ASAN shadow memory mappings. Colima uses QEMU and OrbStack
uses Virtualization.framework — neither can handle ASAN shadow memory,
so ASAN-instrumented binaries crash at runtime. Updated all docs to
warn macOS agents to use Docker Desktop only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Conservative charging: target deadline rate instead of max speed

Always charge at min_a (the minimum current to finish by deadline) instead
of max_safe during cheap hours. This spreads charging evenly over the time
window and avoids overloading the house.

Example: 5 kWh over 8h → steady 6A for ~1.2h
  vs old: burst 16A for ~0.5h then idle (causing load spikes + start/stop)

max_safe remains as a safety cap (never exceed headroom), not a target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Session panel popout: spec and plan

Spec: doc/specs/session-panel-popout.md
Plan: plan.md with 6 implementation steps

Converts session panel from fixed full-screen overlay to left-side
flex panel inside .work-area, orthogonal to chat state (like applet
panel). Resizable, independently togglable, three-column desktop.

commit.message:
Use Playwright to bypass Cloudflare bot protection

StoryGraph is behind Cloudflare which blocks plain HTTP requests with a
JS challenge. Switched from requests.get() to headless Chromium via
Playwright for page fetching. requests is kept for the Gist API call.

- Added playwright to requirements.txt
- Rewrote fetch_page() to use a shared headless browser
- Updated workflow to install Chromium with --with-deps
- Updated README with new setup step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redesign: queries live in pages, not separate views

Eliminate the separate query picker (SPC v v), saved views, and
QueryResultFrame. A query is a ```bloom code block in a regular
page. The page IS the view.

- Users edit queries by editing the page
- Results render inline, interactive (j/k, x toggle, Enter jump, Ctrl-o back)

One concept instead of three (embedded + picker + saved views).

commit.message:
Fix No button reliability, add taunts, try counter, confetti

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: only inherit entity embedding from canonical-name member after dedup

After entity deduplication/renaming, the merged entity was inheriting
embedding_v2 from the first member with any embedding — even if that
member had a different name than the canonical one. This caused stale
embeddings where the vector encoded the original (pre-rename) surface
form rather than the canonical name.

Now only the member whose name matches the canonical name contributes
its embedding. Merged entities that were renamed get embedding_v2=None,
avoiding mismatched name/embedding pairs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Added code idexer (#3)

* Add StewardessMCPServive.Core (.NET 8 ASP.NET Core MCP service)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update RepositoryRoot in appsettings.json

Changed the `RepositoryRoot` property under the `Mcp` section from an empty string to "C:\\Temp". This update specifies a new directory for the repository root, directing the application to use "C:\\Temp" for its repository files.

* Add CORS support and enhance OpenAPI integration

This commit introduces CORS support to allow any origin to call the API, facilitating cross-origin requests from the Open WebUI. It includes enhancements to Swagger/OpenAPI by generating operation IDs for each endpoint, ensuring compatibility with the Open WebUI tool.

Additionally, middleware for CORS is added to the request pipeline, and a new endpoint is created to redirect requests from `/openapi.json` to `/swagger/v1/swagger.json`.

Integration tests have been updated to verify the implementation of CORS headers, operation ID formats, and redirection behavior for the OpenAPI specification. A new method for creating a non-redirecting `HttpClient` has also been introduced in the test helper class for testing redirect responses.

* Fix all compiler warnings (CS8602/CS8604) in test projects

- McpToolHandlerTests: add Assert.NotNull(response) before property
  accesses since DispatchAsync returns McpResponse?
- FileSystemServiceTests: Assert.NotNull(result.Root.Children) before
  NotEmpty; fix Content?.Length null-conditional; make
  AssertNoFileNodes accept TreeNode?
- SearchServiceTests: use ?. null-conditional on RelativePath
- SecurityServiceTests: McpServiceSettings.CreateForTesting params
  changed from string[] = null! to string[]? = null

Build now has 0 warnings, 215 unit tests pass, 35 integration tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix two remaining CS8604/CS1998 warnings in Core

- SecurityService.ConstantTimeEquals: param 'b' changed to string?
  (ConstantTimeEquals already guards null internally)
- Program.cs /openapi.json alias: remove async keyword since there
  is no await; use explicit (HttpContext ctx) parameter instead

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix integration test failures caused by Swashbuckle + minimal API

The (HttpContext ctx) => void minimal-API lambda was visible to
Swashbuckle 6.9, which failed to generate a schema for it and
returned 500 on /swagger/v1/swagger.json, breaking 4 OpenAPI tests.

Fix: add .ExcludeFromDescription() to the /openapi.json alias endpoint.
Also patch two CS8602 warnings in DiscoveryEndpointsTests.cs
(null-conditional on JToken indexer results).

35 integration tests pass, 0 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore CORS, operationIds, /openapi.json alias, and fix build

Three features that were lost from Program.cs:
- AddCors / UseCors (AllowAnyOrigin/Method/Header) for Open WebUI
- CustomOperationIds generating 'Controller_Action' format
- /openapi.json → /swagger/v1/swagger.json redirect alias

Also restored McpTestServer.CreateNonRedirectingHttpClient() helper
that was referenced by OpenApiIntegrationTests but never saved.

146 integration tests pass, 0 build errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Eliminate per-request allocations in route dispatch fallback

- Skip exact-match routes in pattern-match loop (already handled by jump table)
- Reuse a single Dictionary across TryMatch calls instead of allocating per call
- Add TryMatchCore to RouteMatching to support dictionary reuse
- Merge the two fallback loops into one (ALL verb routes handled inline)
- Add allocation-free TryLookupConcat to RouteJumpTable for 'ALL {path}' lookup
- Add two-part Hash(prefix, suffix, seed) to RouteHash for concat without alloc

These changes reduce O(N) dictionary allocations per request to zero in the
pattern-match fallback, and eliminate a string.Concat on every non-exact-match
request. The cumulative GC pressure from these allocations was causing outlier
pauses that permanently raised the all-time-average dispatch metric.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip generate entries with received=0 in listtransactionsmulti

When a wallet address appears only as a staking input in the reward
distribution transaction (tx_idx=1), sent>0 but received==0, causing
a spurious 'generate' entry with amount=0 to be emitted for every block
that address staked in. This flooded the wallet with +0.00 TIME entries.

Add an early-continue when tx_idx<=1 and received==0.

commit.message:
perf: word-based prefix comparison + SWAR slash scanning in EntityPrefixRouter

Three optimizations for the entity prefix router hot path:

1. 64-bit word comparison (#1297/#1299): precompute first 8 bytes of
   each entity slug as a ulong at build time. At resolve time, pack
   the lowered input into a ulong and XOR against stored prefix words.
   Slugs ≤ 8 bytes (most entities) resolve with a single ulong
   comparison — no SequenceEqual, no per-byte loop. Longer slugs
   check the first 8 bytes via word, then SequenceEqual for the tail.

   The match uses branchless combined check:
     diff = slugWord ^ entry.PrefixWord
     combined = diff | (uint)(slugLen ^ entry.Length)
   A single CBZ (ARM64) or JNE (x64) on combined replaces separate
   length and content branches.

2. SWAR slash scanning (#1301): TryParseAndResolve now uses FindSlash()
   which scans 8 bytes per iteration using the standard byte-detection
   trick (XOR with 0x2F broadcast, then (x-0x01..01) & ~x & 0x80..80)
   instead of byte-by-byte IndexOf. Falls back to scalar for the
   < 8 byte tail.

3. ByteLiteral gains Length and PrefixWord fields, eliminating repeated
   Bytes.Length accesses and enabling the word comparison path.

Closes #1297
Closes #1299
Closes #1301

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore ASAN+UBSAN in Docker image, drop multi-arch for AMD64-only

The cloud agent removed ASAN/UBSAN to enable QEMU-based ARM64 cross-compilation,
but this defeated the purpose of iccanalyzer-lite (security analysis requires
sanitizer instrumentation). ASAN shadow memory is incompatible with QEMU
user-mode emulation but works correctly under Docker Desktop's Rosetta 2
on Apple Silicon Macs.

Changes:
- Restore libclang-rt-18-dev in both builder and runtime stages
- Re-enable ASAN+UBSAN in iccDEV cmake flags and iccanalyzer-lite build
- Add ASAN_OPTIONS/UBSAN_OPTIONS env vars to runtime container
- Remove QEMU setup from CI workflow, build linux/amd64 only
- Update docs: macOS agents use Rosetta 2 (not native ARM64)
- Add macOS/Apple Silicon usage section to remote-analysis.prompt.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  EphemeralKeyBackupActions, BankruptcyOverlay, Leaderboard, Timeline,
  and FlightBoard to use react-i18next translations
- Add i18n initialization to test setup for proper test execution
- Add comprehensive i18n test suite (10 tests) covering language switching,
  interpolation, fallback, and HTML lang sync
- All 161 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean
